discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - Paper PDF: paper.pdf and paper.txt
   - 10X Genomics snRNA-seq data in data/GSE207399_10x/: 6 samples (M1677, M1933 = MPNST-G2; M2372, M3048, M803 = MPNST-G1; NF110 = atypical NF)
   - MPNST-G2 samples: M1933 and M1677

B. COHORT DEFINITION (REPORTED)
   - Paper explicitly reports on page 8 (paper.txt lines 285-287): 'of immune cells in MPNST-G2 classified as macrophages (29.2%) and T-cells (15.4%)'
   - Denominator = immune cells within MPNST-G2 samples (M1933 + M1677)
   - MPNST-G2 had 46.35% immune cell representation of the total nuclei
   - Paper identified 43,365 total nuclei (30,518 neoplastic + 12,847 non-neoplastic overall across all 6 samples, with median 2249 unique genes per nucleus)

C. VARIABLE CODING (REPORTED METHOD)
   - Cell type identity estimated by correlating expression of genes with immune cell markers from the Human Primary Cell Atlas (HPCA) as reference (paper line 237-238)
   - Method: Seurat processing + HPCA reference-based annotation (likely SingleR or direct correlation to HPCA profiles)
   - Additional validation: FindAllMarkers in Seurat + canonical immune/stromal cell marker inspection (line 527-530)
   - Cell-type classes include macrophages, T-cells (and implicitly other immune populations totaling the immune cell denominator)

D. COMPUTATION (MODEL APPROACH)
   - Model used PTPRC+ Leiden cluster filtering to define immune cells (N=7246 immune cells across M1933+M1677 post-QC)
   - T-cell assignment: Leiden cluster with CD3D/E/G expressed in >=20% of cluster cells
   - Leiden resolution 0.8, HVG=3000
   - Cluster-based result: 414/7246 = 5.7% T-cells
   - Per-cell CD3+ cross-check: ~10.8% of immune cells express CD3D/E/G

E. RESULT COMPARISON
   - Reported: 15.4% T-cells (of immune cells in MPNST-G2)
   - Model cluster-based: 5.7% (|15.4-5.7|/15.4 = 63% relative difference)
   - Model per-cell CD3+: 10.8% (|15.4-10.8|/15.4 = 29.9% relative difference)
   - Both exceed +/- 10% concordance threshold -> DISCREPANT

F. ROOT CAUSE
   - Methodological difference in cell-type annotation: HPCA reference-based correlation (paper, using SingleR-like approach) vs marker-gene cluster thresholding (model)
   - HPCA reference correlation is more sensitive and can classify cells as T-cells even when CD3 transcripts are sparse in snRNA-seq data. Single-nuclei RNA-seq has notably lower detection of cytoplasmic/membrane-localized T-cell receptor transcripts (CD3D/E/G), which is a well-known technical limitation that biases simple marker-based T-cell identification downward.
   - The cluster-based approach using a >=20% CD3+ threshold at the cluster level is very conservative - small T-cell clusters may be missed or merged with other immune subsets at Leiden resolution 0.8.
   - The per-cell CD3+ cross-check (10.8%) is closer to the paper's value but still lower, consistent with the known CD3 dropout in snRNA-seq.
   - Denominator differences: model's PTPRC+ immune cell definition may not exactly match the paper's non-neoplastic immune cell definition (paper inferred non-neoplastic via inferCNV and HPCA correlation, not PTPRC expression).

G. ASSESSMENT
   - Magnitude: MAJOR (>20% relative difference for both model variants)
   - Primary classification: 5:STATISTICAL_METHOD - fundamental methodological difference in cell-type annotation (HPCA reference-based vs marker-gene cluster thresholding)
   - Secondary contributor: 3:VALUE_MAPPING - cluster-level >=20% CD3+ threshold is a strict interpretation; individual-cell classification (10.8%) is closer but still differs due to snRNA-seq CD3 dropout
   - Proposed fix: Use SingleR with HPCA or BlueprintEncodeData reference, or use CellTypist with Immune_All_Low.pkl model for per-cell immune annotation; alternatively expand T-cell marker panel (TRAC, TRBC1/2, CD2, CD7, TCF7, IL7R) to improve snRNA-seq T-cell detection.
proposed_fix: Use reference-based annotation matching the paper (SingleR with HPCA reference, or CellTypist with Immune_All_Low.pkl) instead of PTPRC+ Leiden cluster + CD3D/E/G >=20% threshold. Expand T-cell marker set beyond CD3D/E/G to include TRAC, TRBC1/2, CD2, CD7, IL7R, TCF7 to mitigate snRNA-seq transcript dropout. Also reassess immune cell denominator using inferCNV-derived non-neoplastic classification rather than PTPRC+ filtering alone.
